factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium Deflation
factors.summary: US-Iran ceasefire agreement and Strait of Hormuz reopening announcement eliminates major war premium that has supported gold. Oil crashed ~4% on the news, a direct proxy for geopolitical de-risking and lower inflation expectations.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD marked lower in early Asia trade on peace deal news (per Investinglive 21:35 & 21:40). Risk-off geopolitical premium typically supports USD; its weakness here suggests equity risk-on and competing safe-haven flows away from gold toward equities.

factors.name: Equity Risk Sentiment Reversal
factors.summary: US stock-index futures jumped 0.7-1.0% (ES/NQ) on Iran deal news; ASX and Japanese stocks set to rally. Risk-on mood typically reduces gold's safe-haven demand and redirects capital to growth assets.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No new TIPS yield data in news window. Pending Fed decisions (Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting this week per MarketWatch 21:00) could shift real yields; event risk remains high but no catalyst yet triggered.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations via Oil/Commodities
factors.summary: Oil down 4%+ on Hormuz reopening; Iranian crude sanctions suspended, flooding market with supply. Lower energy costs and commodity prices will suppress near-term inflation prints and CPI expectations, reducing safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Upcoming FOMC
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first press conference as Fed chair is this week; no guidance yet released. Warsh is an unknown quantity. FOMC outcome will be critical; positioned to cap confidence until decision/guidance released.

factors.name: Central Bank Buying Structural Bid
factors.summary: No news on PBoC, RBI, or other central bank gold purchases in this window. Structural long-term bid remains intact but is not a near-term catalyst for current price action.

factors.name: Physical Demand Seasonality
factors.summary: Mid-June; neither Indian wedding season nor Chinese New Year. Seasonal tailwinds are absent.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU flow data reported in news window.

factors.name: Move Phase Assessment
factors.summary: Initial reaction has already occurred: USD weaker, equities higher, oils lower, within ~4 hours of peace announcement (21:40 GMT to ~01:40 GMT next day). Price action underway; chasing extended moves increases whipsaw risk.

screening.narrative: The surprise US-Iran ceasefire and Strait of Hormuz reopening announcement on 14-15 June triggered an immediate de-risking rally in equities and a sharp reversal of the geopolitical premium embedded in gold over the past 4+ months of war. Oil crashed 4%, the USD weakened, and stock futures jumped on fading Middle East risk. Gold is now facing headwinds from lower inflation expectations (via cheaper energy supply post-Hormuz), a risk-on equity shift, and potential real-yield pressures if the Fed signals caution at Warsh's first FOMC meeting. However, deal execution is uncertain—Trump has already made conflicting statements (Hormuz 'opening very shortly' vs 'Friday' vs ongoing Iran threats), and geopolitical de-escalation could unwind quickly if negotiations fail.
catalysts_ahead: Warsh's first FOMC decision & press conference (this week, date not specified but imminent)
US-Iran deal formal signing ceremony (19 June, Switzerland)
Strait of Hormuz formal reopening timeline (30-day window per MOU)
Potential Israeli-Iran escalation (Iranian threats of retaliation for Beirut strikes remain live as of 20:38 news)
Oil market re-pricing as Iranian supply enters market (intra-week)
US inflation data (CPI/PCE)—likely to show lower prints if oil stays depressed
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran deal collapses, Netanyahu defies Trump on Hezbollah/Gaza ops, or Iran launches retaliatory strike before signing (19 June), geopolitical premium re-inflates sharply and gold reverses higher. Additionally, if Warsh signals hawkish/restrictive policy at FOMC, real yields could rise and gold could extend lower; conversely, dovish surprise could trigger a bullish gold reversal. Short-term move is still in early phase; whipsaw risk is elevated until deal is formally signed and FOMC is concluded.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (Phase 2); wait for a pullback retest of 2420-2430 resistance or technical support flush toward 2375-2385 before adding shorts. Do not chase the sell-off below 2400 into thin early-Asia/pre-London open. Confirm breakdown below 2400 on volume and await FOMC clarity before committing to longer-term shorts.

screening.current_price: ~2425-2435 (inferred from 'gold gains momentum' and 'weekly high' post-Iran deal)

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran Peace Deal)
factors.summary: US-Iran interim agreement announced to reopen Strait of Hormuz, halting 3.5+ month conflict. Reduces inflation shock from oil disruption and lowers Fed tightening urgency. Multiple sources confirm deal to be signed Friday.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI crashed to two-month low near $79.40 from deal announcement. Lower energy prices ease inflation concerns, reducing real yield support for USD and potentially weakening Fed rate-hike expectations, which is bullish for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY dropped to ~99.50 as safe-haven demand faded post-deal. Broad USD weakness across pairs (EUR/USD +0.35% to 1.1610, GBP/USD +0.35%, USD/JPY softening, USD/CAD down). Weaker USD is structurally bullish for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields / 10y TIPS
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data in window, but Treasury market rallied and traders dialed back Fed hike expectations post-deal. Lower real yields (implicit) = bullish for gold. ECB Lagarde cautiously optimistic on inflation relief from geopolitical easing.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equity Rally
factors.summary: Asian equities surge (Kospi +5% circuit halt, SoftBank +12%, Taiwan +2.36%, AUD/JPY +0.5%). Risk-on environment can reduce safe-haven demand for gold, partially offsetting geopolitical bullishness. Gold traded as inflation-relief play, not pure hedge.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Stance
factors.summary: No new FOMC data or Fed speak in window. Ex-BoJ economist noted BOJ hike to 1% Tuesday is locked in regardless of Iran deal; Fed implications unclear but broadly traders expect slower hikes. Awaiting next macro data for clarity.

factors.name: Inflation Data / Expectations
factors.summary: German wholesale prices +5.9% YoY (down from +6.3% prior) but monthly -0.6% (caveat: energy tax cut). No US CPI/PCE in window. Oil shock relief removes near-term inflation tail risk, but 2nd-round effects and services inflation remain per Lagarde.

factors.name: Physical Gold Demand
factors.summary: Singapore removing 5% gold cap on eligible funds (structurally significant incremental demand from Asia's managed capital pools). LBMA considers earlier morning auction for Asian traders. Central bank vaulting announcement by October signals reserve manager interest.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows
factors.summary: No explicit GLD/IAU holdings data in window. Inflow/outflow direction unclear; risk-on sentiment may spur tactical profit-taking vs. safe-haven buying.

factors.name: Competing Safe-Haven (CHF, BTC, USD)
factors.summary: Swiss franc under pressure (risk-on after Iran deal). USD broadly weak. BTC strength and tech rally (SpaceX, Zhipu AI) may offer competing 'risk-on' flows away from traditional safe havens.

key_levels.resistance: 2440-2450 (recent highs, Friday target post-Iran deal confirmation)

screening.narrative: Gold rocketed higher on US-Iran peace deal announcement, easing 3.5-month geopolitical premium and oil shock inflation fears. WTI crashed to two-month lows (~$79.40), reducing real yield pressure and signaling trader expectations for slower Fed hiking. USD Index fell sharply as safe-haven demand evaporated and risk sentiment flipped bullish (Asian equities +5%, AUD/JPY +0.5%). The move is underway and extended in 4-6 hour window; gold now trades as an inflation-relief play rather than pure geopolitical hedge, with structural support from Asian physical demand (Singapore fund cap removal) and potential central bank vault flows. However, near-term pullback risk is rising given rally momentum and equity co-movement.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran peace deal formal signing (Friday, June 20) — execution risk remains high; deal not yet signed
BoJ rate hike to 1% (Tuesday, June 18) — 31-year high, major JPY/carry trade catalyst; locked in regardless of Iran deal
US economic data (next week) — NFP, CPI/PCE, ISM Services — will reset real yield expectations
G7 summit (Évian-les-Bains, France, week of June 17) — Trump tariff threats on France tech tax; geopolitical headline risk remains
ECB speakers this week — Lagarde already signaled caution on 2nd-round inflation; further dovish messaging could cap gold rally
Middle East volatility watch — Israel stating it is not bound by Lebanon ceasefire clause in MOU; execution and compliance risks
screening.risk_warning: If the Iran deal falls apart before Friday signing OR Israel escalates Lebanon operations, geopolitical premium returns sharply and gold could spike further (5-10% upside). Conversely, if equity rally sustains and risk appetite normalizes (DXY recovers, real yields re-steepen), gold could pullback 1-2% to 2410-2420 as safe-haven demand fades. Early Fed speakers this week (Powell, Waller) or hotter-than-expected inflation data would flatten this bullish thesis quickly. Move is already extended; late entry chasing can suffer 30-50 pips of mean reversion.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2/early PHASE_3 (already +1-2% in 6h). Recommend waiting for a pullback to 2410-2420 support before entering long; if support holds on retest, size in with tight stops below 2405. Alternatively, observe for consolidation breakout above 2440 resistance on fresh volume (post-deal signing Friday) before chasing higher. Near-term risk/reward is unfavorable at current levels given extended rally.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation (US-Iran Deal)
factors.summary: US and Iran reached interim MoU to reopen Strait of Hormuz, ending Middle East war. This major de-risking removes a significant inflation/energy-price tail risk that had been bullish for gold as safe-haven. However, initial reaction is mixed: equity rally + oil crash suggest risk-on dominance, which can pressure gold if it shifts from safe-haven to correlation with equities.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Treasury yields have fallen 4+ bps (10y at 4.441% per MarketWatch); with disinflation expectations rising post-Iran deal, real yields likely declining. Lower real yields are the single most bullish factor for gold. This directly supports the $4,340+ move already underway (+2.86% reported in recent data).

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD weakening across the board post-Iran deal; multiple sources report USD lower to start the week. Weaker DXY is structurally bullish for gold. Risk-on sentiment favors carry unwind and softer dollar.

factors.name: Fed Rate-Hike Expectations
factors.summary: Market pricing in only 16 bps of Fed tightening by year-end (vs 24 bps pre-deal per investingLive). Dovish rate repricing is bullish for gold. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh decision looming this week; if dovish, extends tailwind.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: Oil collapsed to 3-month lows near $80 WTI as Hormuz reopening eases supply fears. Lower oil removes inflation pressure, supporting lower real yields. However, equity rally on lower-energy-cost backdrop suggests risk-on shift, which could limit gold's safe-haven appeal despite positive real-yield tailwind.

factors.name: Equity Market Risk-On
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures up 1.3%, SpaceX surging 6%, broad equity strength. Risk-on environment typically reduces gold's safe-haven demand; if equities capture risk appetite, gold may struggle despite lower real yields unless rates continue falling sharply.

factors.name: Recent Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: Gold already up +2.5-2.86% from Thursday lows ($4,024), with +0.39% on 24h, +0.17% on 4h. Move is in early-to-mid phase; breakout from $4,024 to $4,340+ is significant but not yet extended. Momentum is UP.

factors.name: Inflation Data Recent
factors.summary: No hot inflation surprises in the news window. US Empire Fed manufacturing activity surprised weak at +5.7 vs +14.0 expected (prior +19.6), suggesting softer economic data. Weakness in manufacturing supports disinflationary narrative and lower-for-longer rates view, modestly bullish for gold.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (BoJ, ECB)
factors.summary: BoJ hiking this week amid JPY near 160.00; ECB Lagarde welcomes Iran deal but stresses need for further hikes despite peace. Hawkish central banks globally could support USD and higher real yields, offsetting some gold bullish drivers. ECB Kazimir warns against frontloading rate cuts.

factors.name: Upcoming Catalysts: FOMC This Week
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first decision on the agenda this week. Markets are pricing dovishness post-Iran deal, but Warsh could surprise hawkish. High uncertainty caps conviction until decision released.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Central Bank Buying
factors.summary: No recent data on GLD/IAU holdings or PBoC purchases in the news window. Cannot assess institutional positioning changes or structural demand shifts from this snapshot.

screening.narrative: Gold is in a PHASE_2 rally following the US-Iran interim peace deal, which collapsed geopolitical risk premium and triggered a sharp DXY weakness and real-yield decline. The 2.5-2.86% rebound from Thursday's $4,024 lows is driven by lower real yields (10y Treasury -4 bps to 4.441%), dovish Fed repricing (only 16 bps tightening priced by year-end), and a weaker dollar. However, equities are rallying aggressively on lower-oil-price tailwinds and risk-on sentiment, which risks shifting gold from a safe-haven to a correlation play with equities. The move is in mid-phase and has captured initial buyers; vulnerability arises if equity strength persists and real yields stabilize or rise into a more-hawkish-than-expected Warsh FOMC decision this week.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision & Kevin Warsh first guidance (this week, high impact)
BoJ rate decision (likely hold, but forward guidance key for JPY/carry unwind)
ECB decision or updated guidance (divergence with dovish repricing)
Treasury yield direction (any hint of real-yield stabilization could break the rally)
US macro data (NFP, ISM, CPI) — softer data extends gold bid, stronger data risks reversal
Gold ETF inflows/outflows (institutional positioning not visible in current snapshot)
screening.risk_warning: The Iran deal euphoria and equity rally could fade if: (1) geopolitical tensions reignite (Israel signals non-compliance per AlJazeera, or talks collapse); (2) real yields stabilize or tick higher as market reprices the 'lower rates for longer' narrative; (3) Kevin Warsh delivers a hawkish surprise, re-anchoring rate expectations upward; (4) US economic data surprises hot and inflation expectations reaccelerate; (5) risk sentiment rotates back to flight-to-quality and USD strength returns, pressuring gold as DXY reverses. Equities could also correct sharply if earnings disappoint post-euphoria, reverting gold to safe-haven but from a higher and more crowded technical position.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is PHASE_2 (in progress, 2.5% rally already printed). Optimal entry is on pullback/retest to $4,320-4,340 support before pursuing higher targets ($4,400+). Avoid chasing breakouts above $4,380; instead wait for dip-buyers or a 4h retest if momentum stalls. High-impact FOMC this week = cap position sizing until post-decision volatility settles.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Framework / Geopolitical De-escalation
factors.summary: US and Iran reached interim agreement to reopen Strait of Hormuz, setting 60-day negotiation window. This is a major safe-haven unwind story BUT it's simultaneously weakening the USD and lowering rate-hike expectations, both bullish for gold. The net effect is gold-positive via lower real yields and weaker dollar offsetting reduced crisis premium.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Expectations
factors.summary: Fed rate hike expectations immediately pared back post-deal: market now pricing 16 bps of tightening by year-end vs 24 bps before announcement per investinglive. Lower terminal rate expectations reduce real yields, which is strongly bullish for gold. FOMC decision looms this week under new Chair Warsh—major catalyst ahead.

factors.name: US Dollar (DXY)
factors.summary: USD broadly lower to start the week per investinglive wrap, with risk-on flows and reduced safe-haven demand post-Iran deal. Weaker dollar is mechanically bullish for dollar-denominated gold. However, move already underway—USD weakness priced into current gold rally.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Inflation Input)
factors.summary: Brent and WTI plunging to 3-month lows (sub-$80), down 35% from April peaks per multiple sources. Lower energy prices reduce inflation pressures, lowering Fed tightening urgency and real yields—both bullish gold. However, this also removes some inflation-hedge demand for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields (Implied)
factors.summary: Though TIPS yields not explicitly cited, lower nominal Treasury yields (10y down 4+ bps to 4.441% per CNBC) combined with stable/lower inflation expectations implies falling real yields—the single most important bullish factor for gold. Bond market rallying on Iran deal.

factors.name: Nominal Treasury Yields
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield fell 4+ bps to 4.441% per CNBC as Iran deal drives rethink on Fed rate hikes. Lower nominal yields reduce opportunity cost of holding non-yielding gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Safe-Haven Dynamics
factors.summary: Equity futures up 1.3% (S&P 500) in risk-on response to Iran deal. Gold gaining despite risk-on environment suggests dollar weakness and lower yields are dominating the safe-haven unwind. This is unusual but constructive—gold holding gains even as crisis premium fades.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (BoJ, ECB)
factors.summary: BoJ widely expected to hike rates this week per Reuters/FXStreet, but JPY not strengthening (USD/JPY still above 160). ECB hawks (Kazimir, Nagel) calling for frontloading rate hikes despite peace deal. Diverging policy creates cross-currents but no clear directional signal for gold.

factors.name: Economic Data (Empire Fed)
factors.summary: US June Empire Fed Manufacturing index fell to +5.7 vs +14.0 expected (prior +19.6), with new orders collapsing to +3.5 from +22.7. Softening activity could support dovish Fed pivot, but weak data also reduces inflation pressures already falling via oil—mixed signal, slightly negative for gold's inflation-hedge appeal.

factors.name: FX Dynamics (EUR/USD, USD/JPY)
factors.summary: Dollar weakness broad-based across majors per investinglive. USD/JPY holding above 160 despite imminent BoJ hike suggests dollar weakness is generalized, not just yen-specific. Euro and other majors strengthening supports gold rally.

factors.name: Recent Price Action & Positioning
factors.summary: Gold up +2.5-2.86% on the day per FXStreet, rebounding from Thursday's $4,024 low. Move is significant but still within normal volatility given major geopolitical catalyst. Not yet parabolic or extended, but entry at current levels is chasing—Phase 2 scenario.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying & ETF Flows
factors.summary: No specific updates on PBoC purchases or GLD/IAU flows in news window. Structural bid remains but not a fresh catalyst in this 24h cycle.

confirmation.narrative: Gold is rallying strongly (+2.5-2.86%) as the US-Iran peace framework triggers a USD sell-off and lowers Fed rate-hike expectations from 24 bps to 16 bps by year-end. The dominant story is NOT traditional safe-haven demand (equities are rallying 1.3%) but rather falling real yields via lower nominal Treasury yields (10y -4 bps to 4.441%) and reduced Fed tightening urgency as oil collapses below $80. This is a dovish macro repricing that heavily favors gold despite the geopolitical risk premium unwinding. However, the move is already 2+ hours old and well-progressed—this is Phase 2, not a fresh entry point.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision this week under new Chair Kevin Warsh (major unknown—first meeting, policy stance unclear)
BoJ policy decision expected to hike rates (JPY dynamics, USD/JPY above 160)
US-Iran formal signing in Switzerland June 19 (implementation risk, Israel non-compliance risk per news)
Any Fed speakers pre-FOMC (Warsh, Williams, Waller comments on inflation/Iran impact)
Oil inventory data and Hormuz reopening logistics (supply normalization timeline)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) New Fed Chair Warsh signals aggressive hawkish stance at FOMC, re-pricing rate expectations higher and spiking real yields; (2) Iran deal collapses or Israel escalates (headlines mention Israel 'not bound' by Lebanon ceasefire clause); (3) USD reverses sharply higher on flight-to-quality if geopolitical stability proves fleeting; (4) Gold fails to hold $4,240 support, signaling the rally is a short-covering bounce rather than sustained bid. Current move is Phase 2—vulnerable to profit-taking into FOMC uncertainty.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway (+2.86% intraday)—avoid chasing at $4,366. Wait for pullback toward $4,280-4,300 support or consolidation below $4,350 before adding longs, especially with FOMC decision looming this week creating two-way risk.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: 10-year Treasury yield fell ~3 bps to 4.457% on US-Iran deal optimism (per Bloomberg). Lower nominal yields + disinflation narrative from oil collapse = lower real yields, the single strongest gold tailwind.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI crude crashed 4.53% to $79.15 and touched $80.42 on Strait of Hormuz reopening deal (per FXStreet). This eases inflation expectations and reduces Fed rate-hike pressure, supporting gold via lower real yields.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation (US-Iran Deal)
factors.summary: Trump confirmed US-Iran MOU signed; Strait will reopen Friday with oil flowing immediately. This is major risk-off catalyst that typically drives safe-haven bids into commodities like gold as inflation risk dissipates and growth fears ease.

factors.name: USD Index (DXY)
factors.summary: AUD/USD surged 0.46% to 7-week highs (per FXStreet), EUR/USD held firm around 1.1598 after hitting 1.1662 intraday. Softer USD from risk-on sentiment and lower rate expectations is moderately tailwind for gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equities
factors.summary: S&P 500 and Nasdaq broke higher with bullish gaps; SpaceX IPO raised $85.7B; chip stocks surged. Risk-on flows typically coincide with falling real yields and can reduce competitive pressure from equities on gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy Expectations
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh seen as having political honeymoon with Trump; markets are pricing in rate-hold or eventual cuts as inflation narrative softens from oil collapse and geopolitical relief.

factors.name: Gold's Intraday Action
factors.summary: FXStreet reports gold rallied >3% on Monday from lows of $4,218 to $4,351; current price $4,345 is up from that level, confirming move is in progress but not yet overextended.

factors.name: Physical & Structural Demand
factors.summary: No fresh data on central bank buying (PBoC, RBI) or ETF flows in news window; seasonal factors (Indian summer, post-monsoon) not yet peaked.

screening.narrative: Gold surged >3% Monday on US-Iran ceasefire breakthrough, which collapsed oil $4.46/bbl and crushed inflation expectations. Real yields sank as 10y Treasury yield fell 3 bps amid disinflation narrative; Fed rate expectations have shifted dovish as geopolitical risk premium unwound. The move is in early-to-mid phase (PHASE_2), with price already rallied from $4,218 lows to $4,345 current, confirming the catalyst thesis but not yet overextended. Prior bias history shows BUY6 (Haiku 06:21) and BUY7→BUY6 (Sonnet 13:12), so this is a confirmed sustained bullish read, not a flip.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran deal formal signing Friday in Geneva (could derail if talks collapse; currently bid into gold)
Building permits & economic data due Tuesday (could shift Fed expectations if stronger-than-expected)
Fed funds futures repricing as market absorbs lower inflation, lower rates narrative
Oil inventory data mid-week (Strait reopening will show in data; sustained $75–$82 WTI keeps inflation tame)
Earnings reports (John Wiley, La-Z-Boy Tue) unlikely to move macro, but risk sentiment stay-power matters
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran deal stalls or collapses before Friday signing, oil could spike back and real yields could re-steepen, triggering reversal of gold move. If equity market correction accelerates (risk-off), gold could see profit-taking despite lower yields (safe-haven flip). If new CPI/inflation data surprises hot, market reprices rate cuts and gold loses tailwind. If USD breaks higher on growth optimism (opposite of current risk narrative), DXY strength could cap upside.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (Phase 2); avoid chasing above $4,380–$4,400 resistance. Consider adding on retest of $4,300 support or sub-$4,280 pullbacks if real yields hold lower. Best entry is break above $4,380 on fresh close and hold, or fade any intraday spike above $4,400 for tactical short on mean reversion.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk premium collapse
factors.summary: US-Iran deal signed per Trump, Strait of Hormuz reopening Friday. Oil plunged $4.46 to $80.42 (WTI), removing major inflationary threat and safe-haven bid that supported gold above $4,300. FXStreet reports gold rallied 3%+ from $4,218 lows, but the initial war-premium unwind is a headwind.

factors.name: Real yields (implied)
factors.summary: US 10-year Treasury yield fell ~3bps to 4.457% per CNBC as Iran deal drives rethink on Fed rate hikes. Lower nominal yields + falling oil (disinflationary) imply real yields declining or stable, which is structurally bullish for gold.

factors.name: Federal Reserve policy expectations
factors.summary: Iran deal and oil collapse reduce inflation pressure, prompting market to price lower terminal rate. Treasury yields sliding as traders reassess Fed hiking cycle. New Chair Warsh expected to hold steady per CNBC; dovish pivot speculation supports gold. However, Citadel Securities warns higher rates challenge risk assets.

factors.name: US Dollar (DXY) direction
factors.summary: Risk-on mood from Iran deal is weighing on USD safe-haven demand. EUR/USD holding firm around 1.1598-1.1662, AUD/USD strongest major (+0.46%, hitting weekly highs). Weaker dollar is gold-positive, offsetting some geopolitical premium loss.

factors.name: Oil prices
factors.summary: WTI down 4.53% to ~$79.15, Brent plunging on Hormuz reopening. Disinflationary for headline CPI, reduces Fed hiking pressure, indirectly bullish for gold. However, removes immediate crisis premium that had supported $4,300+ levels.

factors.name: Risk sentiment
factors.summary: Equities surging (S&P, Nasdaq broke above 100-hour and 200-hour MAs per Investing.com), SpaceX IPO success, corporate debt issuance $40B+ on bullish mood. Gold acting as risk asset in this regime, not pure safe-haven, benefiting from dollar weakness and lower yields despite geopolitical de-escalation.

factors.name: Inflation outlook
factors.summary: Oil collapse is major disinflationary input; eases headline CPI pressure over next 1-3 months. BNY's Bob Savage notes energy market stress significantly reduced, calming inflation concerns. This supports dovish Fed pricing and gold.

factors.name: Central bank gold buying
factors.summary: No fresh news in 24h window, but structural bid from PBoC, RBI, EM central banks remains multi-year tailwind providing floor under $4,000-$4,200 zone.

factors.name: ETF flows / positioning
factors.summary: No specific GLD/IAU flow data in news. Gold rallied 3%+ from session lows per FXStreet, suggesting some buy-the-dip demand, but unclear if institutional or retail driven.

factors.name: Upcoming catalysts
factors.summary: Building permits data Tuesday, no major Fed speakers or CPI/PCE releases imminent per news scan. Warsh's first FOMC meeting outcome and Iran deal finalization Friday are key near-term events.

confirmation.narrative: Gold is caught in a tug-of-war: the US-Iran peace deal collapsed the geopolitical risk premium that had supported $4,300+, sending oil down 4.5% and removing a key safe-haven driver. However, the same deal is triggering a dovish Fed repricing (lower yields, weaker USD) as disinflationary oil eases hiking pressure. Gold rallied 3%+ off $4,218 session lows to $4,351, suggesting the real-yield/dollar story is winning over geopolitical premium loss. The move is Phase 2 (already 3%+ off lows in past hours), so much of the initial reaction is priced, but the medium-term setup favors further upside if yields and USD continue to soften.
catalysts_ahead: Iran deal signing Friday in Geneva – any delays or complications could reverse risk-on trade
Building permits & housing data Tuesday – weak data supports dovish Fed case
Fed Chair Warsh policy signals – June meeting outcome and any hawkish pivots
Oil price trajectory – sustained sub-$80 WTI vs. bounce back above $85
Equity market sustainability – if stocks roll over, gold safe-haven bid returns
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Iran deal collapses or delays, spiking oil back above $85 and reviving war premium that paradoxically competed with gold's monetary role; (2) Fed Chair Warsh signals unexpectedly hawkish stance, driving real yields sharply higher; (3) USD surges on renewed safe-haven demand (e.g., China-Taiwan flare-up, banking stress); (4) break below $4,218 session low signals broader risk-off rotation out of all assets including gold. Move already 3%+ off lows – late chasers risk buying the top of Phase 2 bounce.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move extended from $4,218 to $4,351 already (+3%); wait for pullback toward $4,300-$4,310 support or retest of breakout zone before adding long exposure. Alternatively, use a break above $4,360 resistance with strong follow-through as confirmation of trend continuation.

reason: building conviction (2 matching cycles, not yet mature)
